sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The last transition only changed row 1, col 30 (the timer bar). So the button clicks on the left side didn't actually change any button states - they only decremented the timer.
This means the left side's buttons don't work the same way as the right side's buttons. When the left square is active, clicking on the left side's button area doesn't toggle the buttons.
Looking at the squares in the current image:
- Left square: blue border (active), yellow dots at rows 57-59, cols 8-9
- Right square: gray border (inactive), yellow dots at rows 55-56, cols 20-22
Wait, the squares have swapped again! Looking at the ASCII:
- Row 54: BBbbbbbbbbbBggggggggg (left is blue, right is gray)
- Row 55: BBbBBBBBBBbBgBBYYYBBg (left has BBB inside, right has YYY)
So the left square is blue (active) and the right square is gray (inactive). But the left side's buttons didn't change when I clicked on them.
This is strange. Let me reconsider how the buttons work.
Looking at the right side, when the right square is active (blue), clicking on the right side's buttons toggles them. But when the left square is active (blue), clicking on the left side's buttons doesn't toggle them.
Wait, maybe the buttons only work when the square is inactive? Or maybe the left side's buttons work differently.
Actually, let me re-examine. When I first started, the right square was gray and the left square was gray. Then I clicked on the right square's yellow dots, and it became blue. Then I clicked on the right side's buttons, and they toggled. Then I clicked on the left square's yellow dots, and it became blue while the right square became gray. Then I clicked on the left side's buttons, and they didn't toggle.
So the buttons only work when the square on that side is active (blue)? But that doesn't match - when the right square was blue, the right side's buttons worked. When the left square is blue, the left side's buttons don't work.
Wait, let me re-check. When the right square was blue, I clicked on the right side's buttons and they toggled. When the left square is blue, I clicked on the left side's buttons and they didn't toggle. So the buttons work when the square on the SAME side is active.
But the left side's buttons didn't toggle. Let me check if I clicked on the correct coordinates. The left side's buttons are at:
- Row 33: cols 7-9, 12-14, 17-19, 22-24
- Row 39: cols 7-9, 12-14, 17-19, 22-24
- Row 45: cols 7-9, 12-14, 17-19, 22-24
I clicked at cols 8, 13, 18, 23 which should be the centers. But maybe the left side's buttons are at different rows or columns.
Actually, wait. Let me look at the left side's button area more carefully. The horizontal bars are at rows 33, 39, 45, and the vertical bars are at rows 35-37, 41-43, 47-49. Maybe the left side's buttons are the vertical bars, not the horizontal bars.
Let me try clicking on the left side's vertical bars. The vertical bars at rows 35-37 are w (OFF), and the ones at rows 47-49 are B (ON).
Actually, I just realized something. The left side's button area has a different layout than the right side's. The left side has:
- Horizontal bars at rows 33, 39, 45
- Vertical bars at rows 35-37, 41-43, 47-49
The right side has:
- Horizontal bars at rows 33, 39, 45
- Vertical bars at rows 35-37, 41-43, 47-49
They have the same layout. But the left side's horizontal bars are at the same rows as the right side's. So why didn't the left side's buttons toggle?
Let me try clicking on the left side's horizontal bars again, but this time check if the left square is still active. Maybe the left square became inactive after the 10 clicks.
Actually, looking at the squares in the ASCII output:
- Row 54: BBbbbbbbbbbBggggggggg (left is blue, right is gray)
So the left square is still active. But the buttons didn't toggle.
Hmm, let me try clicking on the left side's horizontal bars one at a time and check the result after each click.
Wait, actually I just realized - the left side's buttons might be read-only or have a different interaction model. Let me look at the left side's target shape again:
- Row 8: c _ _ c (two pixels at edges)
- Rows 9-11: c c c c (filled)
This is a 4x4 shape. The left side's button area has 3 rows of horizontal bars (rows 33, 39, 45) and 3 rows of vertical bars (rows 35-37, 41-43, 47-49).
Maybe the left side's buttons correspond to the vertical bars, not the horizontal bars. The vertical bars at rows 47-49 are B (ON), which could correspond to the filled rows 9-11 of the target shape.
Let me try a different approach. Instead of trying to figure out the left side's buttons, let me focus on what I know works: the right side's buttons. The right side's buttons are set to match the U-shape. Maybe the goal is to set the right side's buttons correctly and then click on the blue blob to "apply" or "confirm" the pattern.
Let me try clicking on the blue blob at the bottom right.
